title: Finally! Kindle Colorsoft Gets a Dark Mode So You Can Read at Night Without Blinding Anyone
summary: Have you ever tried reading in bed with a bright screen blasting your eyes? It's the worst! For a long time, the Kindle Colorsoft — Amazon's color e-reader — didn't have a dark mode. Dark mode flips the screen so the background is black and the words are white. It's way easier on your eyes in a dark room. The regular Kindle Paperwhite had it, but not the colorful one. That was a big problem for people who love reading at night. Well, guess what? Amazon just announced dark mode is coming to Colorsoft devices! The update starts today and will keep rolling out over the next few weeks. You can even download it yourself if you don't want to wait. The coolest part? You can choose which screens go dark. Maybe you want your books in dark mode but your notebooks in light mode. That works great on the Kindle Scribe Colorsoft, which is like a digital notebook. And don't worry — any colorful highlights you made in your books will stay the same color in dark mode. If you've been waiting to buy a Colorsoft until it had dark mode, your wait is over. There are a few models: the basic Colorsoft, the Signature Edition with wireless charging, a Kids version with a fun cover, and the Scribe for note-taking. Now you can read all night without waking anyone up!
why_it_matters: Being able to read at night without a bright screen helps you enjoy books longer without waking up siblings or straining your eyes, making bedtime reading cozy and private.

keywords.explanation: The first Kindle e-reader from Amazon that can show book covers and pictures in color.
A screen setting that uses a black background with white letters to be easier on the eyes in dim light.
A small electronic device used for reading digital books, like a tiny computer screen that looks like paper.
A popular black-and-white Kindle model that already had dark mode before this update.
A Kindle model that lets you write notes with a pen, like a digital notebook.
A way to power up a device by just placing it on a special pad, without plugging in a cable.

questions.question: What does dark mode do on a screen?
options: Makes the background black and words white
questions.correct_answer: Makes the background black and words white

background_read: E-readers like the Kindle use special E Ink screens that look more like real paper than a phone or tablet, which is why they are so comfortable for reading books.
The Kindle Paperwhite (a popular black-and-white e-reader) already had dark mode, so many people were waiting for the color Colorsoft to get the same night-friendly feature.
Many apps and devices now include dark mode because it helps reduce eye strain in low light and makes reading more comfortable before bed.
Article_Structure: WHO: Amazon (the company that makes Kindle) and the owners of Kindle Colorsoft devices.
WHAT: Amazon announced and began releasing a dark mode update for its color e-reader, the Kindle Colorsoft, with extra choices for different screens.
WHERE: On all Kindle Colorsoft devices worldwide, including the Scribe and Kids versions.
WHY: Because people had been asking for a night-reading mode to make the colorful e-reader easier on the eyes in dark rooms, matching the Paperwhite's feature.

perspectives.description: People who love reading in bed are excited because the new dark mode protects their eyes and makes it easier to read without disturbing others.
Some who bought the Colorsoft when it first came out might have been annoyed that a basic feature like dark mode was missing and only arrived later.
Adding dark mode makes the Colorsoft more competitive with other e-readers and tablets that already offered this feature.
